channel.title: Python on Pavan Chavali
channel.description: Recent content in Python on Pavan Chavali

item.title: From E-Waste to Edge Server: Salvaging a 2012 Dell All-in-One for Local-First Automation
item.description:
Have you ever asked your smart speaker to “Play my Morning Focus playlist on Spotify,” only to have it confidently blast a random death metal mix at 7:00 AM?

If you rely on Google Home or Alexa for your daily routines, you already know the dirty little secret of modern IoT: the “smart” part is often just a cloud algorithm guessing what you want.

The “Real Talk” Architecture: Local Control, Deterministic Execution

Before we get into the step-by-step story of my struggle, let’s talk about the solution. What I built is simple: I declared independence from the cloud for this specific routine. I took an old, dormant desktop and turned it into a headless, local-first automation server.

item.title: Revenue Radar: How I Built an AI Strategy Agent Using Gemini and Salesforce (and Cut Token Costs by 70%)
item.description:
Everyone is building chatbots right now. It’s the “Hello World” of the AI era!

But here is the dirty secret of Enterprise AI: Latency and Tokens are the new technical debt.

If you simply paste a Python demo into a Salesforce Flow, you aren’t building an agent—you’re building a generic “chat” interface that burns cash and hits timeouts.

I learned this the hard way. I set out to build Revenue Radar, an agent that analyzes massive 40-page earnings calls.
